$description: Ferrari — Italian motoring heritage on a cinematic black canvas, Rosso Corsa accent and the yellow shield highlight. Editorial-serif display, clean grotesque body. Derived from getdesign.md/ferrari/design-md (luxury automotive, chiaroscuro editorial). MIT.

accent.$description: Rosso Corsa — Ferrari's iconic racing red, primary accent fill.

accent-hover.$description: Rosso Corsa pressed — deeper carmine for active/hover state.

highlight.$description: Modena yellow — the heraldic shield background, used sparingly for inline highlights and badges.

black.$description: Ink — near-pure black, the dominant cinematic canvas.

ink.$description: Body ink on black canvas = warm-tinted cream-white for readability.

graphite.$description: Body strong on dark — emphasized paragraphs, lead text in warm cream.

steel.$description: Muted — sub-headings, breadcrumbs, secondary text on dark surfaces.

silver.$description: Muted soft — captions, fine-print on dark.

fog.$description: Hairline border on dark surfaces, slightly warm-toned.

paper.$description: Canvas — pure cinematic black, the brand-defining drama surface.

paper-2.$description: Surface card — feature cards, content cards on the black canvas.

off-white.$description: On-dark text — warm cream that echoes Maranello heritage.

off-white-2.$description: On-dark soft — footer body text, secondary labels.

surface-dark.$description: Code editors, model showcase cards, footer — same as canvas.

surface-dark-soft.$description: Code block backgrounds inside dark cards.

surface-soft.$description: Section dividers, soft band backgrounds — barely lifted from canvas.

surface-cream-strong.$description: Strongest surface — selected tabs, emphasized bands.

accent-teal.$description: Secondary product accent — status dots, terminal indicators (cool counterpoint to Rosso Corsa).

font-family.$description: Editorial pairing — classical display serif with Italian brio (Optima/Cormorant/EB Garamond) + clean grotesque body (Inter/Helvetica Neue). JetBrains Mono for code.

font-weight.$description: Display sizes use weight 700 (bold) per Ferrari's classical wordmark register — the serif at 700 reads as confident and editorial.

radius.$description: Ferrari uses 4px on CTAs/buttons (sharper, more mechanical than Claude), 8px on content cards, 12px on hero containers. card defaults to 8.
